module: Module 8 - AI, Machine Learning, Analytics, and Data Services

sections.title: Core Idea
points: AWS provides managed AI, machine learning, analytics, and search services.
Use prebuilt AI services when you need fast results.
Use custom ML platforms when building your own models.
sections.exam_tip: AWS exams often ask which service matches a business use case.

sections.title: Artificial Intelligence vs Machine Learning
points: Artificial Intelligence (AI) = systems performing tasks that mimic human intelligence.
Machine Learning (ML) = subset of AI where systems learn patterns from data.
ML learns from historical data and predicts on new data.
sections.exam_tip: Rules = classical programming | Learns from data = ML

sections.title: Training vs Inference
points: Training = teaching a model using historical data.
Inference = using trained model to make predictions.
Example: Train on spam emails, infer on new emails.
sections.exam_tip: Build model = Training | Use model = Inference

sections.title: Generative AI
points: Creates new text, images, code, audio, and more.
Often powered by large foundation models.
Used for chatbots, assistants, summarization, coding help.

sections.title: Foundation Models
points: Large pre-trained models trained on broad datasets.
Can perform many tasks with prompts or fine-tuning.
Used in modern generative AI solutions.
sections.exam_tip: Large reusable AI model = Foundation Model

sections.title: Amazon Bedrock
points: Managed service providing access to multiple foundation models.
Single API for generative AI development.
sections.exam_tip: Need generative AI models quickly = Bedrock

sections.title: Amazon SageMaker AI
points: Managed platform to build, train, and deploy custom ML models.
Used by data scientists and ML engineers.

sections.title: SageMaker JumpStart
points: Provides prebuilt models and starter ML solutions.
Speeds up ML experimentation and deployment.

sections.title: Amazon Q Business
points: Generative AI assistant for enterprise knowledge search.
Connects to internal business data sources.
sections.exam_tip: Ask company knowledge questions = Amazon Q Business

sections.title: Amazon Q Developer
points: Helps with code generation, troubleshooting, AWS guidance.
sections.exam_tip: Developer productivity AI = Amazon Q Developer

sections.title: Amazon Comprehend
points: Sentiment analysis, key phrase extraction, entity recognition.

sections.title: Amazon Lex
points: Builds chatbots and conversational voice/text interfaces.

sections.title: Amazon Textract
points: Extracts text, tables, and forms from documents.

sections.title: Amazon Personalize
points: Used for product or content suggestions.

sections.title: Amazon Kendra
points: Used for searching documents, websites, FAQs.

sections.title: Streaming and Analytics
points: Kinesis Data Firehose = deliver streams to S3, Redshift, OpenSearch.
AWS Glue = ETL and data catalog.
Amazon Athena = SQL queries on S3.
Amazon EMR = big data with Hadoop/Spark.
sections.exam_tip: Streaming = Kinesis | SQL on S3 = Athena | Dashboards = QuickSight

sections.title: ETL vs ELT
points: Move data from sources, clean it, then store it.
sections.exam_tip: ETL is a classic exam term.

sections.title: Common Exam Scenarios
points: Need transcript from call recording = Transcribe
Need generative AI model access = Bedrock
sections.exam_tip: Match use case keywords to service names.

sections.title: Common Exam Traps
points: Athena queries S3 data, not transactional databases.
QuickSight visualizes data, not stores data.
SageMaker builds models, Bedrock uses foundation models.
